title: stackalloc и ref struct: стек, ограничения

cards.prompt: stackalloc int[100] assigns to a Span<int> without unsafe and allocates 0 managed-heap bytes (it is on the stack)
cards.code: using System; static (bool,int) StackAllocMeasure(){ long a0=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); Span<int> s=stackalloc int[100]; for(int i=0;i<100;i++) s[i]=i; long a1=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); return ((a1-a0)==0, s[99]);} var(noHeapAlloc,last)=StackAllocMeasure(); Console.WriteLine($"stackallocNoHeapAlloc={noHeapAlloc} lastElem={last}");

cards.prompt: The recommended conditional stackalloc pattern keeps a small buffer on the stack (0 heap bytes) but puts a large one on the heap
cards.code: using System; static long Alloc(int n){ long a0=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); Span<byte> b = n<=1024 ? stackalloc byte[n] : new byte[n]; b[0]=1; long a1=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); return a1-a0;} long smallBytes=Alloc(100); long largeBytes=Alloc(100000); Console.WriteLine($"smallOnStack={smallBytes==0} largeOnHeap={largeBytes>0}");

cards.prompt: A ref struct can implement IDisposable (C# 13) and works with the using statement
cards.code: using System; ref struct R : IDisposable { public static int disposed=0; public void Dispose()=>disposed++; } static void Use(){ using var r=new R(); } Use(); Console.WriteLine($"refStructDisposed={R.disposed==1}");
